description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly pale, off-white coloration and subtle hints of brown and gray. Its surface is rough and uneven, featuring natural indentations, crevices, and a few angular edges. The stone appears to be solid and dense, with no visible artificial modifications or carvings. There are faint traces of mineral veining and mottling, giving it a slightly marbled appearance. The weathering is moderate, with some smoother areas and others showing granular texture, suggesting exposure to the elements. This stone would be commonly found as a natural obstacle or decorative element in urban parks, sidewalks, or landscaped areas.
description: A rough, pale off-white stone with irregular shape, subtle veining, and natural weathering. Its surface is uneven, with both smooth and granular textures.
description_view_0: This view shows a rounded side with a prominent overhang and a mix of smooth and rough textures.
description_view_1: This angle highlights a more blocky, angular face with visible brownish veining and a flatter surface.
description_view_2: This side presents a tapered, elongated profile with subtle mottling and a less pronounced overhang.
description_view_3: This view reveals a corner with a small cavity or hole and a mix of rough and smoother patches.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or natural obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle mineral veining and mottling with irregular, natural distribution; no artificial carvings.
attribute_weathering: Moderately weathered with a mix of smooth and granular surfaces, indicating exposure to rain and foot traffic.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other stones; may contrast with paved surfaces but integrates well in landscaped settings.

license_info.creator_display_name: UQ School of Earth and Environmental Sciences
